Kelp_Notes: Saw sargassum, the kelp in the intertidal look very unhealthly damaged blades and blub, looks like its already senescing and dying.
Epibiont_Epifauna_and_Epiphyte_Notes: Saw no growth.
Juvenile_Salmon_Notes: Saw pink salmon, school size 3m^3, they were feeding right at the surface in the inner edge of the kelp / at the lower intertidal.
Forage_Fish_Notes: School size of unknown forage fish was 2m^3
Additional_Notes_From_Map: High density area in the lower intertidal/subtidal, kelp was good health. In the intertidal the kelp health seemed to decline to moderate health. Definite decline in kelp health through the channel. the kelp in the nearshore/intertidal were the most unhealthly with tissue damage, missing/damaged blades. Closer to shore = warmer temps + increased dessication/insolation stress?

Kelp_Notes: Looks more unhealthly than high density because of low tides and it going dry/warm.
Epibiont_Epifauna_and_Epiphyte_Notes: Saw no growth.

Kelp_Notes: Kelp was at the surface during low tide out to about the marker buoy. Every size class is well represented, a large distribution of a spectrum of sizes between 0.33-4.0m. Kelp in shallow water was more unhealthy, i.e. missing all blades
Epibiont_Epifauna_and_Epiphyte_Notes: Saw a few bull kelp pieces with brown filamentous algae growing. Saw way more kelp crabs on bull kelp clearly eating blades than last week.
Juvenile_Salmon_Notes: 1 m^3 school of chinook or coho
Forage_Fish_Notes: Unknown fish could very possibly be anchovy but was difficult to tell the difference between anchovy and herring underwater.
Additional_Notes_From_Map: Saw a lot of pisasters, but much more in the low density area. No signs of mass wasting disease. A lot more red algae, i.e. turkish towel and rainbow seaweed, growing south of the bridge on rip rap. A lot of fish in the high density kelp area. Large schools of shiner perch, herring and sand lance observed. The kelp in the shallow nearshore was moderately damaged, with some having lost all blades. Our new hypothesis is that when kelp spores are seeding, some start growing too shallow to sustain a full healthly life cycle, so they begin to senescence prematurely when low tides begin, i.e. when the kelp has grown enough to be very cose to the surface.

Kelp_Notes: Other seaweed/kelp species becoming much more prominent. Kelp observly not as large as the high density area. Had more kelp w/ fully missing blades. Blade ends have damage, but overall kelp looks pretty vibrant.
Epibiont_Epifauna_and_Epiphyte_Notes: Saw kelp crabs on blades, a lot more than last week.
Forage_Fish_Notes: Multiple size classes of sand lance were everywhere. Saw herring just south of the bridge.

Kelp_Notes: Kelp was less abundant than other sites but appeared in similar condition to low density area. Overall smaller in size.

Additional_Notes_From_Map:
Nearshore: almost all kelp lost all of their blades. A lot of epiphytic alga growth, mostly long filamentous and peach-colored feathery alga. 

Eddy: more bryozoa growth on the stipe and bulb and especially down by the holdfast, which is the depth that the highly colonized understory kelps area.
